dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used dc_upper (Donchian Channel Upper) as proxy for 30-day high breakout. Entry condition 2 represents retracement back below breakout high within 2-3 bars using prev_high. Volume threshold uses sma_20 per parameters. Stop loss set to 1 ATR equivalent (approximately 1% on most assets). Exit after 5 bars uses hold_days_max. Take profit at 50% retracement distance is difficult to express in rule format without additional lookback data; exit prioritizes stop loss and time-based exit. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per signal specification, though entry_short remains empty pending directional confirmation.

backtest_rule.reason: Used dc_upper (Donchian Channel Upper) as proxy for 30-day high breakout. Entry condition 2 represents retracement back below breakout high within 2-3 bars using prev_high. Volume threshold uses sma_20 per parameters. Stop loss set to 1 ATR equivalent (approximately 1% on most assets). Exit after 5 bars uses hold_days_max. Take profit at 50% retracement distance is difficult to express in rule format without additional lookback data; exit prioritizes stop loss and time-based exit. Direction marked CONTEXT_DEPENDENT per signal specification, though entry_short remains empty pending directional confirmation.

source_rule_text: A trader should use short-term setups based on recent price breakouts to identify lower-risk entry conditions by compressing the timeframe of analysis.
